title: NOC 74202 – Air transport ramp attendants | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 74202 professionals are the ground-support specialists who ensure the safe and efficient turnaround of aircraft at Canada’s airports. As a TEER 4 occupation, Air Transport Ramp Attendants (commonly known as "Rampies") manage the critical window between an aircraft's arrival and departure. In 2026, as Canada’s aviation sector fully transitions to Electric Ground Support Equipment (eGSE) and expands its cargo capacity to support global e-commerce, these workers are classified as Essential Aviation Infrastructure Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway and the BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS) stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level4Major Group74 – Mail and message distribution, transport equipment operators and maintenanceSub-Major Group742 – Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workersMinor Group7420 – Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workersNOC Code74202

duties: Baggage & Cargo Handling: Load and unload passenger luggage, air mail, and freight using belt loaders and specialized cargo containers (ULDs).
Aircraft Marshaling: Direct aircraft to and from gate positions using hand signals or light sticks to ensure safe clearance from ground obstacles.
Ground Support Equipment: Operate pushback tugs, water and lavatory service trucks, and ground power units (GPUs) to support aircraft turnaround.
Safety & Security: Position passenger stairs or jet bridges; monitor aircraft for any visible damage or fluid leaks during the walk-around inspection.
Environmental Support: (Seasonal) Perform aircraft de-icing operations using specialized fluid application vehicles to ensure flight safety in winter.
Aircraft Grooming: Clean and maintain the interior and exterior of aircraft, including restocking cabin supplies and cleaning flight decks.
Communication: Maintain constant radio contact with flight crews and ramp coordinators to ensure on-time departures.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.

Certifications (Mandatory):RAIC (Restricted Area Identity Card): Must pass a high-level federal security background check to work in secure airport zones.DA (D-Aviation) License: A specialized airport driver's license is required to operate vehicles on the airside.VHF Radio License: Required for communicating with Ground Control and flight crews.
RAIC (Restricted Area Identity Card): Must pass a high-level federal security background check to work in secure airport zones.
DA (D-Aviation) License: A specialized airport driver's license is required to operate vehicles on the airside.
VHF Radio License: Required for communicating with Ground Control and flight crews.
Training: Extensive on-the-job training (2–4 weeks) is provided by the airline or ground handling firm.
Physical Fitness: High physical stamina is mandatory; workers must be able to lift up to 32 kg (70 lbs) repeatedly and work outdoors in all weather conditions.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Ramp Leads or those specialized in Aircraft De-icing often earn annual salaries between $55,000 and $75,000 inclusive of overtime and shift premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the aviation sector will face a significant "Retirement Wave" over the next decade. As Canada expands its regional airport network and integrates more automated logistics, the demand for human operators to manage the physical safety and high-touch aspects of the ramp ensures long-term job security through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for ramp attendants in 2026 is Very Good, particularly in major international hubs. In Ontario (Pearson), British Columbia (YVR), and Quebec (Trudeau), the rapid recovery of international tourism and a 15% increase in dedicated freighter flights have created a persistent labor shortage. According to 2026 aviation industry reports, ground handling companies like Swissport, ATS, and Menzies are actively utilizing the Temporary Foreign Worker Program (TFWP) to fill gaps in 24/7 ramp operations.
Advanced Aviation Tech: Work with the latest automated baggage handling systems and zero-emission electric pushback tugs.
Rapid Career Progression: This role is the primary entry point for careers in Flight Operations, Aircraft Maintenance, or Airport Management.
Travel Benefits: Many ramp positions with major airlines (Air Canada, WestJet) offer significant flight discounts and travel perks for employees and their families.

Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 4 role is a recognized category for Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP) and the current federal 2026 immigration pilots, offering a streamlined route to Permanent Residency.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, targeting essential transport and infrastructure roles.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A direct route for ramp agents currently employed in British Columbia for at least 9 months.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for those with a valid job offer and RAIC clearance working at YYC or YEG airports.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for workers with a job offer from a designated airport service provider in the Atlantic provinces.
